title: Applied ML Baseline
description: Build a compact baseline project that turns raw text into a measurable prediction workflow. This is the fast lane for proving you can move from dataset to model to simple service.
whyItMatters: A strong baseline project proves you understand data shape, evaluation, and simple production packaging. That foundation still matters when you later work with larger frontier systems.
skillsLearned: Using pre-trained models from Hugging Face
prerequisites: Complete the Python for AI Engineers lesson

steps.title: Set up your environment
steps.description: Create a new Python project with a virtual environment. Install transformers, torch, and pandas.
steps.tips: Use `pip install transformers torch pandas` — transformers is the Hugging Face library.

steps.title: Load a pre-trained sentiment model
steps.description: Use the Hugging Face pipeline API to load a sentiment analysis model. `from transformers import pipeline; classifier = pipeline('sentiment-analysis')` — that is two lines of code to get a working model.

steps.title: Test with sample text
steps.description: Try the classifier on 10-15 sample sentences: product reviews, social media posts, customer feedback. Observe the predictions and confidence scores.

steps.title: Build a batch processor
steps.description: Write a function that reads a CSV of customer reviews (create a sample CSV or download one from Kaggle), classifies each review, and outputs a new CSV with the original text plus the predicted sentiment and confidence score.

steps.title: Analyse the results
steps.description: Use Pandas to summarise: what percentage of reviews are positive/negative/neutral? What is the average confidence? Are there reviews where the model seems wrong? Why?
steps.tips: Look at edge cases: sarcasm, mixed sentiment, very short texts. These reveal model limitations.

steps.title: Wrap it in an API
steps.description: Create a simple FastAPI endpoint that accepts text and returns the sentiment. Test it with curl or the auto-generated docs.

deliverables: Python script that classifies sentiment from a CSV file
FastAPI endpoint for real-time sentiment analysis
Summary analysis showing sentiment distribution and model accuracy observations

rubric.description: The model correctly classifies at least 80% of test samples with reasonable confidence scores.
The script handles CSV input and output cleanly, with proper error handling for malformed text.
The FastAPI endpoint accepts text input and returns structured JSON with sentiment and confidence.
The summary includes meaningful observations about model strengths, weaknesses, and edge cases.
